original_hypothesis.title: Protocol-Wide Denial of Service via Admin Tax Cut Misconfiguration
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If the admin sets the taxCut parameter to 0, all core protocol functions (minting, trading, forging) will permanently revert due to division-by-zero errors.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Multiple contracts (NukeFund, EntityForging, EntityTrading) use `taxCut` as a divisor to calculate fee splits (e.g., `msg.value / taxCut`). The `setTaxCut` admin functions do not enforce a minimum value of 1. If an admin accidentally or maliciously sets `taxCut` to 0, every transaction that processes fees will revert, bricking the entire protocol.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Missing require(_taxCut > 0) in setter, leading to msg.value / taxCut reverting

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Missing zero-check on a state variable used as a divisor

operational_questions: Is there a UI validation preventing the admin from inputting 0?
validation_requirements: Verify that setting taxCut to 0 causes minting to revert

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin key is compromised or admin makes a typo setting taxCut to 0
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: User attempts to mint, trade, or forge
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Transaction reaches fee calculation and attempts division by 0
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Transaction reverts, halting all economic activity on the protocol

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete halt of all revenue-generating protocol functions
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Easy (admin sets it back to >0), but causes severe temporary disruption

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: setTaxCut(0) transaction followed by massive revert rates
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Sudden drop in protocol activity to zero

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
